2605.00618.title: Is Textual Similarity Invariant under Machine Translation? Evidence Based on the Political Manifesto Corpus
2605.00618.paper_summary: We investigate the extent to which cosine similarity between paragraph embeddings is invariant under machine translation, using the Manifesto Corpus of over 2,800 political party platforms in 28 languages translated to English via the EU eTranslation service. Rather than measuring translation-induced semantic shift directly we measure the stability of pairwise similarity relationships across embedding models, and use inter-model disagreement on original-language text as a calibrated invariance threshold. This yields a per-language non-inferiority test for four hypotheses about how translation interacts with embedding choice, with verdicts that distinguish languages where translation demonstrably preserves semantic structure from those where it demonstrably degrades it and from those where the available evidence does not resolve the question. The framework is corpus- and pipeline-agnostic and extends naturally to downstream tasks. Applied to our data, it identifies ten languages with translation invariance and four with detectable distortion.
2605.00618.paper_summary_zh: 我們調查了段落嵌入之間的餘弦相似度在機器翻譯下的變異程度，使用了超過2,800個政黨平台的Manifesto Corpus，這些平台以28種語言翻譯成英語，並通過歐盟eTranslation服務進行處理。<lb/>我們並不是直接測量翻譯引起的語義轉變，而是測量嵌入模型之間的成對相似性關係的穩定性，並使用原語言文本的模型間不一致性作為校準的不變性閾值。<lb/>這為四個假設提供了每種語言的非劣性測試，這些假設關於翻譯如何與嵌入選擇互動，並且其裁決區分了翻譯明顯保留語義結構的語言與翻譯明顯降低語義結構的語言，以及那些可用證據無法解決該問題的語言。<lb/>該框架與語料庫和流程無關，並自然擴展到下游任務。<lb/>應用於我們的數據，它識別出十種具有翻譯不變性的語言和四種具有可檢測扭曲的語言。
2605.00618.author: Daria Boratyn et.al.
2605.00618.authors: Daria Boratyn, Damian Brzyski, Albert Leśniak, Wojciech Łukasik, Maciej Rapacz, Jan Rybicki, Wojciech Słomczyński, Dariusz Stolicki
